findings.category: Logic Bug — Silent failure of all lens subagents
findings.summary: --model flag silently breaks the full multi-lens /drykiss review: all 6 subagents fail with 'No model resolved'
findings.detail: At line 336–338: `const modelMap = options.model ? undefined : await resolveAllModels(...)`. When the user passes --model=haiku, modelMap is set to undefined. At line ~350, `modelMap?.get(lens)` returns undefined for every lens, causing every subagent to short-circuit with errorMessage: 'No model resolved'. The synthesis then runs on 6 error entries, produces an empty findings array, and the user sees a report with 0 findings and no indication that all lenses failed.
findings.suggestion: When --model is provided, resolve it via findModelByHint and create a Map mapping every lens to that model. Or delegate to a new resolveAllModelsWithHint function in subagent-runner.ts that accepts an optional hint.

findings.summary: review-command.ts has zero test coverage; no review-command.test.ts exists
findings.detail: This file contains ALL business logic for the DRYKISS review system. The critical --model flag bug above would have been caught by even a basic integration test. The JSON parsing functions bridge LLM output to user-facing review results and their silent-failure-on-parse behavior is completely untested.
findings.suggestion: Create src/review-command.test.ts covering: parseFindingsJson with valid/invalid/malformed JSON, parseSynthesisJson, gatherDiffs error path, handleKissCommand with no files, executeDrykissReviewTool, and the --model flag paths that are currently broken.

findings.summary: subagent-runner.ts has zero test coverage; no subagent-runner.test.ts exists
findings.detail: This file manages Pi subagent session lifecycle: resolveModel, resolveAllModels, runLensSubagent (session creation, prompt execution, result extraction, abort handling, cleanup). If runLensSubagent leaks sessions or fails to handle aborts, it can leave the IDE in a broken state.
findings.suggestion: Create src/subagent-runner.test.ts. Mock createAgentSession and test: resolveModel with config hint, no models (throws), fallback to first available, runLensSubagent error path, abort signal disposal, extractAssistantText variants.

findings.category: Zero test coverage — LLM resolution and retry logic
findings.summary: llm.ts has zero test coverage; no llm.test.ts exists
findings.detail: This file contains model resolution (resolveModelSmart with 5-step priority chain), model matching (findModelByHint), and LLM calling with quota/auth error recovery that retries once with a user-selected model. The error recovery and retry logic is especially risky — if error classification is wrong, users get confusing error loops.
findings.suggestion: Create src/llm.test.ts testing: resolveModelSmart with explicit hint, per-lens config, fallback; findModelByHint exact/partial/no match; callLLM success/ quota error / auth error paths.

findings.category: Dead code — modelHint parameter completely ignored
findings.summary: Ternary in runLensReview has identical branches; --model flag silently ignored for all single-lens commands
findings.detail: Both branches call `resolveModel(ctx, cwd, lens)` identically. The options.modelHint flag is evaluated but never passed to resolveModel (which doesn't accept a hint parameter). Every single-lens command ignores the user's model choice. Flagged by 4 of 6 reviewers.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a real hint-aware call: use findModelByHint on available models when modelHint is provided, falling back to resolveModel. Also add a modelHint parameter to resolveModel in subagent-runner.ts.

findings.summary: resolveModel duplicates resolveModelSmart from llm.ts with a divergent implementation
findings.detail: llm.ts:resolveModelSmart implements 5-step priority (hint→config→interactive→fallback). subagent-runner.ts:resolveModel reimplements 3-step (config→interactive→fallback), skipping hint and default config. When one changes, the other won't stay in sync. Both can return different models for identical inputs. Flagged by 3 reviewers.
findings.suggestion: Delete resolveModel from subagent-runner.ts and import resolveModelSmart from llm.ts. Add a thin wrapper if the throw-vs-undefined contract differs.

findings.category: Duplicated detectLanguage with diverging extension maps
findings.summary: detectLanguage is defined in both edit-tracker.ts (15 extensions) and git-diff.ts (30+) with inconsistent coverage
findings.detail: edit-tracker.ts is missing: kt, rb, swift, cs, cpp, c, h, scala, json, sh, bash, zsh, dockerfile. When a user edits a .json, .rb, or .sh file, edit-tracker records language as null while git-diff correctly identifies it. Any new extension requires updates to two maps that will inevitably diverge again.
findings.suggestion: Extract detectLanguage into a shared module (src/detect-language.ts) using git-diff.ts's more complete mapping as canonical. Import from both files.

findings.category: DRY violation — five near-identical command handlers
findings.summary: Five single-lens handlers share ~90% identical code (~175 lines of duplication)
findings.detail: All five follow: parseArgs→getChangedFiles→empty check→ensureDefaultPrompts→gatherDiffs→loadConfig→conditional gatherContents→try/catch runLensReview→format→notify. Only the lens string, projectIndex flag, empty message, and title differ. Adding a new lens requires copy-pasting the entire pattern.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a single parameterized function handleLensReview(lens, args, ctx, pi, opts?). Each exported handler becomes a one-liner delegating to it.

findings.summary: handleDrykissCommand has 7+ distinct responsibilities in one function
findings.detail: The function: parses args, fetches changed files, validates file count, loads config, creates prompts, resolves 6 models, spawns 6 subagents, runs synthesis, persists results, builds report, notifies user. Error handling is a single catch block that loses context about which stage failed.
findings.suggestion: Break into focused stages: resolveModels, runLenses, synthesize, persistAndReport. Each returns typed results with delegated error handling.

findings.category: Dynamic imports where static imports suffice
findings.summary: Three await import('./subagent-runner.js') call sites instead of one top-level static import
findings.detail: Lines 194, 234, and 336 all dynamically import from subagent-runner.js. Lines 336 and 346 even import from the same module separately. This adds latency, prevents tree-shaking, and obscures the dependency graph. No conditional or lazy-loading justification exists.
findings.suggestion: Replace all with a single top-level `import { resolveModel, resolveAllModels, runLensSubagent } from './subagent-runner.js'`.

findings.summary: handleConfigCommand uses a long if-else chain; adding a subcommand requires editing the function
findings.detail: Seven subcommands plus unknown-subcommand error are handled by sequential if-blocks. Each branch is 7–25 lines. Adding a new subcommand means increasing cyclomatic complexity. Individual subcommand testing requires the whole function.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a command registry: `const subcommands: Record<string, Handler> = { show, 'set-default': ..., ... }`. Lookup and delegate: `(subcommands[subcommand] ?? showUsage)(tokens, ctx)`.

findings.summary: parseFindingsJson and parseSynthesisJson have no tests despite being the LLM→user bridge
findings.detail: Both functions extract JSON from AI output via regex, coerce types, and silently return empty/default on failure. Malformed AI output is silently dropped with no user feedback. This is the most fragile integration point in the system and is completely untested.
findings.suggestion: Test: valid/markdown-wrapped/invalid/non-array JSON for parseFindingsJson. Valid/missing-fields/invalid JSON for parseSynthesisJson including severity count verification.

findings.summary: gatherDiffs catches all errors and replaces diff with '(diff unavailable)' with no logging
findings.detail: If git diff fails due to corrupted repo, permission error, or shell timeout, the error is completely hidden. The reviewer then analyzes stale or missing diffs, potentially producing incorrect findings.
findings.suggestion: Add `console.error('[pi-drykiss] Failed to get diff for', file.path, err)` inside the catch block.

findings.summary: When synthesis JSON parsing fails, a generic fallback is returned with no error logged
findings.detail: If parseSynthesisJson returns null, a hardcoded fallback with summary 'Synthesis returned non-JSON output...' and 0 findings is returned. The user gets no actionable feedback if the synthesis LLM errored due to quota, timeout, or bad response.
findings.suggestion: Log the raw synthesis output and parsing error. Consider ctx.ui.notify with 'warning' severity instead of silent fallback.

findings.summary: loadConfig catches ALL errors including JSON parse failures and permission errors, returning defaults silently
findings.detail: A malformed or permission-denied config.json silently resets to defaults. If the config is later saved, the corrupted config is overwritten, destroying custom lens model settings.
findings.suggestion: Differentiate ENOENT (use defaults) from other errors (log warning, use defaults, notify user).

findings.summary: getAllSourceFiles and getProjectIndex implement identical source-dir discovery and walk patterns
findings.detail: Both functions repeat a 15-line bootstrap of checking src/lib/app/packages existence, falling back to '.', then walking recursively. The logic is identical except for collection targets. Updating one walk requires touching both.
findings.suggestion: Extract `async function resolveSourceDirs(cwd: string): Promise<string[]>` shared between both functions.

findings.summary: buildKissDryInjectionBlock is a pass-through to buildAutoInjectBlock with a redundant null check
findings.detail: The function checks empty files then delegates. But buildAutoInjectBlock already handles empty files (returns a checklist string) and the caller checks for null. This abstraction adds a layer with zero leverage.
findings.suggestion: Delete buildKissDryInjectionBlock. Import buildAutoInjectBlock directly in handleBeforeAgentStart.

findings.category: Runtime validation diverges from type system
findings.summary: validLenses array includes 'synthesis' which is not part of the ReviewLens type union
findings.detail: ReviewLens = 'simplicity' | 'deduplication' | 'clarity' | 'resilience' | 'architecture' | 'tests' | 'all'. validLenses includes 'synthesis' and excludes 'all'. The type system and runtime validation are out of sync.
findings.suggestion: Either add 'synthesis' to ReviewLens or remove it from validLenses. Derive one from the other to keep them in sync.

findings.summary: buildAutoInjectBlock accepts 'language' field on each file but never uses it
findings.detail: The parameter type includes `language: string | null` but only `f.path` is accessed. Callers must provide a language value that has no effect. This widens the interface unnecessarily.
findings.suggestion: Narrow the parameter type to `{ files: ReadonlyArray<{ path: string }> }`. If language is intended for future use, add a comment.

findings.summary: Uses 'as keyof typeof' cast on an unvalidated runtime string, bypassing type checking
findings.detail: `config.lensModels?.[lens as keyof typeof config.lensModels]` casts without validating the lens is a valid key, silently returning undefined for invalid inputs. Callers get no autocomplete for valid lens names.
findings.suggestion: Accept the proper union type instead of a generic string. Define a reusable LensKey type and reference it consistently.

findings.summary: Three config subcommand handlers (interactive, confirm, context-mode) follow identical structure
findings.detail: Each: parse→loadConfig→validate→set property→saveConfig→notify. ~42 lines of near-identical code with different property names and valid values.
findings.suggestion: Extract `async function toggleConfigField(ctx, cwd, field, validValues, value, label): Promise<void>`.

findings.summary: listReviews catches all JSON parse errors silently, hiding data corruption from the user
findings.detail: If a review file has a partial write or encoding issue, the user sees fewer reviews with no explanation. The array may be empty or incomplete with zero feedback.
findings.suggestion: Log a warning: `console.warn('[pi-drykiss] Skipping corrupt review:', entry)`. Optionally report skip count to the user.

findings.summary: calculatePopupWidth computes complex widths that are negated by final clamping to [40, 120]
findings.detail: The 15-line function accounts for prefix/gap/padding/margin/help text but the result is always clamped to [40, 120]. A simpler heuristic produces equivalent results.
findings.suggestion: Replace with: `Math.min(Math.max(Math.max(...items.map(i => visibleWidth(i.label || '')), visibleWidth(title)) + 4, 40), 120)`.

findings.summary: JSON_OUTPUT_INSTRUCTIONS and SYNTHESIS_JSON_INSTRUCTIONS share ~60% identical formatting rules
findings.detail: Both contain near-identical 'Output ONLY the JSON. No markdown code fences' rules, severity values, and empty-output handling. When one changes, the other likely needs the same change.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared BASE_JSON_RULES constant and append format-specific fields.

findings.summary: EditTracker interface exists with exactly one implementation and no planned alternates
findings.detail: The 7-line interface and 45-line factory wrap a simple Map-based state machine. Nothing depends on the interface — consumers use the returned object structurally. Abstraction for its own sake.
findings.suggestion: Remove the EditTracker interface. Export the factory function directly; TypeScript infers the type structurally.

findings.summary: set-lens with no lens argument shows 'Invalid lens: undefined' instead of usage help
findings.detail: When tokens[1] is undefined, validLenses.includes(undefined) is false, producing 'Invalid lens: undefined. Valid: simplicity, deduplication, ...' instead of the usage message.
findings.suggestion: Add guard: `if (!lens) { ctx.ui.notify('Usage: /drykiss-config set-lens <lens> <model>', 'warning'); return; }`

findings.summary: The array ['src', 'lib', 'app', 'packages'] is defined in two different functions
findings.detail: Subsumed by the medium-severity duplicate directory-walking finding. This is the smallest instance of that duplication — a module-level constant would fix it.
findings.suggestion: Define `const SOURCE_DIRS = ['src', 'lib', 'app', 'packages']` at module level and reference from both functions.

summary: CRITICAL: The --model flag is completely broken — in the full /drykiss path it causes all 6 lens subagents to silently fail (modelMap=undefined), and in single-lens commands it's ignored (identical ternary branches). This means every --model=sonnet invocation produces an empty or misleading review.
